#import libraries
library(wordcloud)
## Loading required package: RColorBrewer
library(RColorBrewer)
library(tm)
## Loading required package: NLP
library(kableExtra)
library(readr)
library(ggplot2)
## 
## Attaching package: 'ggplot2'
## The following object is masked from 'package:NLP':
## 
##     annotate
library(stringr)
library(tidyselect)
library(tidyverse)
## -- Attaching packages --------------------------------------- tidyverse 1.3.0 --
## v tibble  3.1.0     v dplyr   1.0.5
## v tidyr   1.1.3     v forcats 0.5.1
## v purrr   0.3.4
## -- Conflicts ------------------------------------------ tidyverse_conflicts() --
## x ggplot2::annotate() masks NLP::annotate()
## x dplyr::filter()     masks stats::filter()
## x dplyr::group_rows() masks kableExtra::group_rows()
## x dplyr::lag()        masks stats::lag()
library(readr)
TextData <- read_csv("TextData.csv")
## Warning: Missing column names filled in: 'X1' [1]
## 
## -- Column specification --------------------------------------------------------
## cols(
##   X1 = col_double(),
##   date = col_date(format = ""),
##   agenda = col_logical(),
##   speechnumber = col_double(),

##   party = col_character(),
##   party.facts.id = col_double(),
##   chair = col_logical(),
##   terms = col_double(),
##   text = col_character(),
##   parliament = col_character(),
##   iso3country = col_character()
## )

## 19592 agenda 1/0/T/F/TRUE/FALSE Clinical Negligence 'TextData.csv'
## 19593 agenda 1/0/T/F/TRUE/FALSE Clinical Negligence 'TextData.csv'

## See problems(...) for more details.
TextData<-TextData[sample(1:nrow(TextData), 10000),]
TextData_features=colnames(TextData)
TextData_features
##  [1] "X1"             "date"           "agenda"         "speechnumber"  
##  [5] "speaker"        "party"          "party.facts.id" "chair"         
##  [9] "terms"          "text"           "parliament"     "iso3country"
features_continous<-vector()
for (i in TextData_features){
  print(paste("The unique values for feature",i, "are :",length(unique(TextData[[i]]))))
}
## [1] "The unique values for feature X1 are : 10000"
## [1] "The unique values for feature date are : 857"
## [1] "The unique values for feature agenda are : 1"
## [1] "The unique values for feature speechnumber are : 696"

colnames(TextData)

##  [9] "terms"          "text"           "parliament"     "iso3country"
str(TextData)

Gentleman acknowledge that some doctors will be left without a job, or does he agree with the Sec"| __truncated__ ...

library('dplyr')
na_count1 <-sapply(TextData, function(y) sum(length(which(is.na(y)))))
na_count1 <- data.frame(na_count1)
na_count1
##                na_count1
## X1                     0
## date                   0
## agenda             10000
## speechnumber           0
## speaker                4
## party                343
## party.facts.id       465
## chair                  0
## terms                  0
## text                   0
## parliament             0
## iso3country            0
TextData<-select(TextData,-agenda)
TextData<-drop_na(TextData)
table(TextData$party)
## 
##       APNI        Con        DUP       GPEW        Lab     LibDem PlaidCymru 
##          2       3281         79          5       4938       1033         44 
##    Respect       SDLP        SNP       UKIP        UUP 
##          2         28        111          5          7
#delete.stop.words(TextData$text, stopwords_regex)
attach(TextData)
table(TextData$iso3country)
## 
##  GBR 
## 9535
TextData$party<-as.factor(TextData$party)
TextData$Text_Length<-nchar(TextData$text,allowNA = T)

hist(TextData$Text_Length, col = rainbow(7))

ggplot(TextData, aes(x=Text_Length, fill=party))+
  geom_histogram()+
  stat_bin(bins = 30)
## `stat_bin()` using `bins = 30`. Pick better value with `binwidth`.
## Warning: Removed 231 rows containing non-finite values (stat_bin).

## Warning: Removed 231 rows containing non-finite values (stat_bin).

  #facet_wrap(~party)
Debates<-TextData%>%
  group_by(party)%>%
  summarise(Number_Debates=n())
kable(Debates, "html") %>%
    kable_styling() %>%
    scroll_box(height = "200px")
party Number_Debates
APNI 2
Con 3281
DUP 79
GPEW 5
Lab 4938
LibDem 1033
PlaidCymru 44
Respect 2
SDLP 28
SNP 111
UKIP 5
UUP 7 
Text_Corpus<-Corpus(VectorSource(TextData$text)) 
#Text_Corpus<-Corpus(VectorSource(TextData$text)) 
print(Text_Corpus[1:2])
## <<SimpleCorpus>>
## Metadata:  corpus specific: 1, document level (indexed): 0
## Content:  documents: 2
inspect(Text_Corpus[1:3])
## <<SimpleCorpus>>
## Metadata:  corpus specific: 1, document level (indexed): 0
## Content:  documents: 3
## 
## [1] I am grateful to the hon. Gentleman and sorry that we did not reach that part of the debate. In Committee, he cited the example of a car accident in which one of the people involved is unconscious and the other person is a doctor, and asked whether the doctor should not do what he can to look after the injured person. I considered that carefully in discussions with officials, parliamentary counsel and others and those discussions confirmed that that area is sufficiently covered by section 5 of the Mental Capacity Act 2005, which will come into effect next April. It provides that if a person acts in connection with the care or treatment of a person who lacks capacity in respect of the matter in question and it is in his or her best interest for that treatment to take place, the person providing the care or treatment does not incur any liability that he would not have incurred if the injured person had been able to consent. That does not prevent a claim from being made if the person has been negligent in providing the care or treatment, but in respect of the provision of that care or treatment, it is sufficiently covered under section 5 of the Mental Capacity Act 2005. I hope that that sufficiently reassures the hon. Member for Montgomeryshire (Lembit Öpik).
## [2] I will happily give way to the hon. Lady and then to the hon. Gentleman.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    
## [3] What I would say is that decisions on whether or not drugs should be available are taken by the National Institute for Health and Clinical Excellence. I am afraid that I cannot make a commitment for a meeting, but I am happy to look into the case that the hon. Gentleman mentions and to correspond with him about it.
toSpace <- content_transformer(function(x, pattern) {return (gsub(pattern," ",
x))})
# Apply it for substituting the regular expression given in one of the former answers by " "
Text_Corpus<- tm_map(Text_Corpus,toSpace,"[^[:graph:]]")
## Warning in tm_map.SimpleCorpus(Text_Corpus, toSpace, "[^[:graph:]]"):
## transformation drops documents
# the tolower transformation worked!
Text_Corpus <- tm_map(Text_Corpus, content_transformer(tolower))
## Warning in tm_map.SimpleCorpus(Text_Corpus, content_transformer(tolower)):
## transformation drops documents
Text_Corpus_Clean<-tm_map(Text_Corpus, tolower)
## Warning in tm_map.SimpleCorpus(Text_Corpus, tolower): transformation drops
## documents
Text_Corpus_Clean<-tm_map(Text_Corpus_Clean, removeNumbers)
## Warning in tm_map.SimpleCorpus(Text_Corpus_Clean, removeNumbers): transformation
## drops documents
Text_Corpus_Clean<-tm_map(Text_Corpus_Clean, removePunctuation)
## Warning in tm_map.SimpleCorpus(Text_Corpus_Clean, removePunctuation):
## transformation drops documents

Text_Corpus_Clean<-tm_map(Text_Corpus_Clean,removeWords,stpw1)
## Warning in tm_map.SimpleCorpus(Text_Corpus_Clean, removeWords, stpw1):
## transformation drops documents
Text_Corpus_Clean<-tm_map(Text_Corpus_Clean,removeWords,stwp2)
## Warning in tm_map.SimpleCorpus(Text_Corpus_Clean, removeWords, stwp2):
## transformation drops documents
Text_Corpus_Clean<-tm_map(Text_Corpus_Clean,removeWords,stpw3)
## Warning in tm_map.SimpleCorpus(Text_Corpus_Clean, removeWords, stpw3):
## transformation drops documents
Text_Corpus_Clean<-tm_map(Text_Corpus_Clean,removeWords,stpw4)
## Warning in tm_map.SimpleCorpus(Text_Corpus_Clean, removeWords, stpw4):
## transformation drops documents
Text_Corpus_Clean<-tm_map(Text_Corpus_Clean,stemDocument)
## Warning in tm_map.SimpleCorpus(Text_Corpus_Clean, stemDocument): transformation
## drops documents
Text_Corpus_Clean<-tm_map(Text_Corpus_Clean,stripWhitespace)
## Warning in tm_map.SimpleCorpus(Text_Corpus_Clean, stripWhitespace):
## transformation drops documents
inspect(Text_Corpus_Clean[1:2])
## <<SimpleCorpus>>
## Metadata:  corpus specific: 1, document level (indexed): 0
## Content:  documents: 2
## 
## [1] grate hon gentleman reach debat committe cite car accid peopl involv unconsci person doctor ask doctor injur person consid care discuss offici parliamentari counsel discuss confirm area cover mental capac april person act connect care treatment person lack capac respect matter question treatment place person provid care treatment incur liabil incur injur person consent prevent claim person neglig provid care treatment respect provis care treatment cover mental capac hope reassur hon member montgomeryshir lembit öpik
## [2] happili hon ladi hon gentleman
Text_dtm<-DocumentTermMatrix(Text_Corpus_Clean)

Most frequent words based on party Con

Con_Cloud<-which(TextData$party=="Con")
wordcloud(Text_Corpus_Clean[Con_Cloud], min.freq = 350,
          colors=brewer.pal(8,"Accent"),random.order = F)

Most frequent words based on party LibDem

LibDem_Cloud<-which(TextData$party=="LibDem")
wordcloud(Text_Corpus_Clean[LibDem_Cloud], min.freq = 80,
          colors=brewer.pal(8,"Accent"),random.order = F)

Lab_Cloud<-which(TextData$party=="Lab")
wordcloud(Text_Corpus_Clean[Lab_Cloud], min.freq = 20,
          colors=brewer.pal(8,"Accent"),random.order = F)

DUP_Cloud<-which(TextData$party=="DUP")
wordcloud(Text_Corpus_Clean[DUP_Cloud], min.freq = 20000,
          colors=brewer.pal(8,"Accent"),random.order = F)

Most frequent words based on party UUP

UUP_Cloud<-which(TextData$party=="UUP")
wordcloud(Text_Corpus_Clean[UUP_Cloud], max.words=10000,
          colors=brewer.pal(8,"Accent"),random.order = F)

Most frequent words based on party SNP

SNP_Cloud<-which(TextData$party=="SNP")
wordcloud(Text_Corpus_Clean[SNP_Cloud],max.words=10000,
          colors=brewer.pal(8,"Accent"),random.order = F)

Most frequent words based on party GPEW

GPEW_Cloud<-which(TextData$party=="GPEW")
wordcloud(Text_Corpus_Clean[GPEW_Cloud],min.freq = 10,
          colors=brewer.pal(8,"Accent"),random.order = F)

Most frequent words based on party APNI

#library(wordcloud2) 
APNI_Cloud<-which(TextData$party=="APNI")
wordcloud(Text_Corpus_Clean[APNI_Cloud],min.freq = 200,
          colors=brewer.pal(8,"Accent"),random.order = F)

#wordcloud2(Text_Corpus_Clean[APNI_Cloud], size=1.6, color='random-dark')

Most frequent words based on party SDLP

Most frequent words based on party Respect

Respect_Cloud<-which(TextData$party=="Respect")
wordcloud(Text_Corpus_Clean[Respect_Cloud],min.freq = 1,
          colors=brewer.pal(8,"Accent"),random.order = F)

Most frequent words based on party PlaidCymru

wordcloud(Text_Corpus_Clean[PlaidCymru_Cloud],max.words=10000,
          colors=brewer.pal(8,"Accent"),random.order = F)

Corelation Analysis

Inspecting word frequency

inspect(Text_dtm[1:5,1:5])
## <<DocumentTermMatrix (documents: 5, terms: 5)>>
## Non-/sparse entries: 5/20
## Sparsity           : 80%
## Maximal term length: 5
## Weighting          : term frequency (tf)
## Sample             :
##     Terms
## Docs accid act april area ask
##    1     1   1     1    1   1
##    2     0   0     0    0   0
##    3     0   0     0    0   0
##    4     0   0     0    0   0
##    5     0   0     0    0   0
inspect(Text_dtm[10:25,10:15])
## <<DocumentTermMatrix (documents: 16, terms: 6)>>
## Non-/sparse entries: 3/93
## Sparsity           : 97%
## Maximal term length: 8
## Weighting          : term frequency (tf)
## Sample             :
##     Terms
## Docs claim committe confirm connect consent consid
##   10     0        0       0       0       0      0
##   11     0        0       0       0       0      0
##   12     0        1       0       0       0      0
##   13     0        0       0       0       0      0
##   14     0        0       0       0       0      0
##   15     0        0       0       0       0      0
##   16     0        0       0       0       0      0
##   17     0        0       0       0       0      0
##   23     0        1       0       0       0      0
##   25     0        2       0       0       0      0

Visualizing the most commong words in the speeches

wordcloud(Text_Corpus_Clean,min.freq = 100,
          colors=brewer.pal(8,"Set1"),random.order = F)

Identifying the most 10 occuring words in the speeches

Text_dtm_matrix<-as.matrix(Text_dtm)
Text_matrix_sorted<-sort(colSums(Text_dtm_matrix),decreasing = T)
head(Text_matrix_sorted,n=10)
##    hon govern member  peopl friend minist   hous   year   work   time 
##  11893   7980   7364   6690   4991   4954   4699   4346   4058   3576
Text_df<-data.frame(words=names(Text_matrix_sorted),Freq=Text_matrix_sorted)
kable(head(Text_df,n=20), "html") %>%
    kable_styling() %>%
    scroll_box(height = "200px")
words Freq
hon hon 11893
govern govern 7980
member member 7364
peopl peopl 6690
friend friend 4991
minist minist 4954
hous hous 4699
year year 4346
work work 4058
time time 3576
issu issu 3559
point point 3346
local local 3233
gentleman gentleman 3157
debat debat 2976
servic servic 2889
support support 2841
countri countri 2816
state state 2674
secretari secretari 2192 
summary(Text_df$Freq)
##     Min.  1st Qu.   Median     Mean  3rd Qu.     Max. 
##     1.00     1.00     1.00    26.77     5.00 11893.00
wordcloud(Text_df$words,Text_df$Freq, min.freq = 100,
          colors=brewer.pal(8,"Set2"),random.order = F)

Hypothesis Under test

H1: Conservative women MPs initiate more debates than Labour women MPs. H2: Female Conservative MPs mention sexual harassment, rape and domestic violence more in their debates, while female Labour MPs concern more about topics such as equal pay for equal work, education equality, and paternal leave. H3: Female Labour MPs are more likely to interfere in the debate than female Conservative MPs.

Finding Association between the frequent words

findAssocs(Text_dtm, c('member','work','rape','sexual'),c(.5,.5,.3,.45))

##  hon 
## 0.77 
## 
## $work
## numeric(0)

Investigating Frequent words from Labour party

Text_Corpus_Lab<-Text_Corpus_Clean[Lab_Cloud]
Lab_dtm<-DocumentTermMatrix(Text_Corpus_Lab)
Lab_dtm_matrix<-as.matrix(Lab_dtm)
Lab_matrix_sorted<-sort(colSums(Lab_dtm_matrix),decreasing = T)
head(Lab_matrix_sorted,n=10)
##       hon    member     peopl    govern    friend      work      hous      year 
##      6889      3605      3431      3217      3156      2385      2237      2159 
## gentleman      issu 
##      1958      1893
Lab_df<-data.frame(words=names(Lab_matrix_sorted),Freq=Lab_matrix_sorted)
summary(Lab_df$Freq)
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
##     1.0     1.0     2.0    20.3     6.0  6889.0
Lab_df%>%
  select(words,Freq)%>%
  filter(words %in% c("women","educ","govern"))
##         words Freq
## govern govern 3217
## educ     educ  587
## women   women  422
Lab_df%>%
  select(words,Freq)%>%
  filter(words %in% c("sexual","harassment","rape","domestic","violence"))
##         words Freq
## sexual sexual   67
## rape     rape   36
Lab_df%>%
  select(words,Freq)%>%
  dplyr::filter(words %in% c("work","equal","educ","leave","paternal"))
##       words Freq
## work   work 2385
## educ   educ  587
## equal equal  202
Text_Corpus_Con<-Text_Corpus_Clean[Con_Cloud]
Con_dtm<-DocumentTermMatrix(Text_Corpus_Con)
Con_dtm_matrix<-as.matrix(Con_dtm)
Con_matrix_sorted<-sort(colSums(Con_dtm_matrix),decreasing = T)
head(Con_matrix_sorted,n=10)
##    hon govern member minist  peopl   hous   year friend   time  point 
##   3419   3307   2519   2311   2183   1749   1515   1471   1261   1169
Con_df<-data.frame(words=names(Con_matrix_sorted),Freq=Con_matrix_sorted)
summary(Con_df$Freq)
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
##    1.00    1.00    2.00   15.95    6.00 3419.00
Con_df%>%
  select(words,Freq)%>%
  filter(words %in% c("women","sexual","exploit","rape","domestic","violence"))
##           words Freq
## women     women  130
## sexual   sexual   32
## rape       rape   26
## exploit exploit   22
